entry.title: Seize the Full Value of Web App Security Investment? (Key Metrics You Can’t Avoid)
entry.content:
Did you know that the average cost of a data breach in 2024 has increased by 10% from last year? Now imagine that cost multiplied by the number of vulnerabilities you might not even be aware of. Yet many companies are throwing money at security solutions without a solid strategy, leaving them vulnerable and unsure if their investments are truly effective. Let me be clear, fast-growing businesses can’t afford this uncertainty to sustain their position in the market.   

In this high-speed digital world, risks are evolving just as quickly as our technologies. So, it’s high time for businesses to rethink their approach and take control by embedding the right metrics into their security strategies.  By doing so, firms can’t just proactively defend against threats but also leverage these metrics to gain a competitive advantage.  

As someone who has navigated the shifting sands of tech for over 24+ years, I can assure you that these metrics can be a game-changer for your business. Now is the time to shift your focus from mere outputs – like the number of firewalls or alerts to the outcomes that truly matter. Even studies have shown that businesses with robust security protocols not only mitigate risks but also build trust and drive sustainable revenue growth.  

Let’s dig into the key metrics that you can’t afford to miss while evaluating your web application security performance 

A List of Essential Metrics to Maximize Your Web App Security Value 

When you navigate the complexities of your web application security, these metrics will serve as your compass, guiding you toward meaningful investments and ultimately, a more secure future. By carefully assessing these critical areas, you can transform your security investments into measurable business value. So, roll up your sleeves and turn these insights into actionable strategies:  

  • A Level of Readiness 

With the help of this metric, you can assess your organization’s preparedness to handle security incidents. By evaluating your readiness, you can identify gaps in training, tools, and processes to ensure that your team is agile and equipped to respond confidently. A high readiness level means quicker, more efficient incident responses which will ultimately reducing potential losses. 

  •  An Incident's Frequency and Impact 

It is one of the metrics that businesses often overlook. By closely understanding how often security incidents occur and their severity allows you to prioritize resources more effectively. If you notice an uptick in incidents, it might signal the need for enhanced security measures or training. Moreover, it can help you shift from a reactive to a proactive security posture, significantly lowering the costs associated with breaches. 

  • Mean Time to Detect (MTTD) 

Speed is highly imperative while dealing with cybersecurity. The quicker you can detect anomalies, the better your chances of mitigating damage. That’s where, MTTD can help you discover your efficiency and make it up-to-mark accordingly. A lower MTTD means your security systems are functioning effectively, allowing you to allocate resources more efficiently and reduce the overall impact of incidents on your organization. 

  • Mean Time to Resolve (MTTR) 

Once an incident is detected, how quickly can you resolve it? Do you know a lower MTTR translates to less downtime and a smoother customer experience, which is invaluable for maintaining trust? By strategically evaluating this metric, you can streamline your incident response processes, reducing the financial impact of incidents and enhancing user satisfaction. 

  • Mean Time to Contain (MTTC) 

How long does it take you to stop a threat from spreading? This metric measures how swiftly you can contain a breach. A low MTTC means you can contain the breach and prevent the spread of threats, which is essential for protecting sensitive data. A high MTTC rate means the threat is running wild. By timely monitoring this metric, you can improve your containment strategies and implement measures early-on. In turn, you can ensure even when breaches occur, their impact is limited. 

  • False Positive Rate 

Not all alerts signal a threat. This metric helps you measure the accuracy of your security tools. A high false positive rate can lead to wasted resources and time. On the other hand, if you Keep this rate low you can reduce unnecessary panic and optimize your security team’s focus on real issues. This metric can be a great help to refine your detection tools, ensuring that your team spends time addressing genuine threats rather than chasing false alarms. 

  • Code Coverage 

This metric assesses how much of your code is being tested for vulnerabilities. Higher coverage means fewer blind spots, leading to a more secure web application development. By regularly evaluating code coverage, you can identify areas for improvement in your web app development process, ultimately reducing the risk of security vulnerabilities in your applications. 

  • Return on Security Investment (ROSI) 

Ultimately, you want to see that your investments pay off. ROSI quantifies the financial benefit of your security measures and help you justify your budget and strategy. A high ROSI means your investments are paying off. A low ROSI means you may need to reevaluate your strategies. This way, you can make informed decisions about future investments, ensuring that every dollar spent on security contributes to your bottom line. 

  • Compliance Adherence Rate 

Are you meeting the standards set by regulators? Because staying compliant isn’t just about avoiding fines; it’s about building trust with your customers. That’s where this metric measures your compliance with industry regulations. A strong adherence rate can enhance your brand reputation, making you a preferred choice for customers who prioritize security. On the other hand, a low adherence means you are playing with fire.  

  • Intrusion Attempts 

Monitoring the number of intrusion attempts provides insight into the threatscape. As high number might indicate that you are a target. This way you can understand these patterns and strengthen your defences proactively. Furthermore, you can tailor your security measures to meet the specific threats your organization faces, maximizing your resources and improving overall security posture. 

Build Risk-Resilient Web Apps That Thrive in a Complex Cyber Sphere 

Remember, security is not merely a checkbox – it is the backbone of a reliable web application development services. So, it’s highly imperative to evaluate whether your security measures are truly worthwhile or just a pain point on your investment. 

For that, you can utilize these key metrics as your guide to fine-tune your strategies and ensure that your AppSec investments are not only justified but also integral to your success.  

So, what are you waiting for? Embed these metrics in your development process and create applications that deliver impact while standing resilient against cyber threats. Because a secure web app is the foundation of sustained success.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget: QRC3 2024 Release
entry.content:
Purpose

This blog introduces the latest updates of Custom Widgets features within SAP Analytics Cloud, as part of the QRC3 2024 release.

Overview

1. Custom Widget: Showing unbooked data values

Previously, the query result returned for the custom widget doesn't include unbooked data. However, displaying unbooked values is crucial. Hence, we're introducing this feature that incorporates unbooked data in the query results which can be utilized by custom widgets. this enhancement allows customers to effectively use unbooked data in their custom widgets.

2. Custom Widget: Support for calculated and account-based dimensions

In the optimized story experience, you can now seamlessly create and utilize calculated or account-based dimensions for custom widgets. This feature is essential for customers who wish to combine dimensions, group them, or create account-based ones for use in custom widgets.

3. Custom Widget: Increasing the number of the hosted custom widget in SAC to 50

Beforehand, SAC user was able to host until 25 Custom Widget per tenant, with the QRC3 release we increased the number to 50 in order to offer more flexibility for custom widget developer to extend their visualisation and easily hosted into SAC and into their Stories.

For further information, visit our SAP Analytics Cloud Community pages to find more product information, best practices, and more. 

Check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to see more upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

Thanks for reading!

entry.title: SuccessFactors Onboarding: The New "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" Step
entry.content:
SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding continues to evolve, providing organizations with robust tools to ensure a seamless and efficient onboarding experience. One of the latest enhancements is the introduction of the "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" step in the Process Variant Manager (PVM). This feature allows organizations to better organize, streamline, and customize tasks for new hires, ensuring a smoother transition into their roles.

Now with the Upcoming 2H 2024 Release, you can configure the Primary New Hire Tasks and Secondary New Hire Tasks steps to customize where standard and custom tasks appear in the Onboarding program. You can also attach a Document Flow for the New Hire Tasks step to either of these steps.

The "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" Step: An Overview

The introduction of the "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" step offers an enhanced way to manage tasks for new hires. This feature differentiates between essential tasks (Primary Tasks) that are crucial for all new hires and supplementary tasks (Secondary Tasks) that may only be relevant for specific groups or roles.

Key Benefits of the "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" Step

  1. Enhanced Task Management and Prioritization:
    • By categorizing tasks as either Primary or Secondary, HR teams can ensure that new hires prioritize critical activities first. This helps streamline the onboarding process, reducing confusion and ensuring compliance with mandatory requirements.
  2. Customized Onboarding Experience:
    • Secondary tasks allow for a more tailored onboarding experience, catering to the unique needs of different employee groups. This ensures that new hires receive relevant information and training specific to their roles, increasing engagement and readiness for the job.
  3. Flexibility and Scalability:
    • The ability to configure tasks in the PVM based on various criteria, such as location, department, or role, provides HR teams with the flexibility to adapt the onboarding process to evolving business needs. It also allows for easy scalability as organizations grow and expand.
  4. Improved Compliance and Reporting:
    • With a clear distinction between mandatory and optional tasks, HR teams can easily track and report on task completion. This ensures compliance with legal and organizational requirements and provides visibility into the onboarding progress of new hires.

Prerequisites

  • A process variant flow for your Onboarding program that includes a Primary New Hires Tasks step and a Secondary New Hire Tasks step.
  • A custom task that you want to add to your Onboarding process variant.
  • Configure a business rule scenario for the process variant.

Configuring the "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" in PVM

To configure the "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" step in the PVM, follow these steps:

Create or Edit a Process Variant:

You can either create a new process variant or edit an existing one. Select the relevant process variant for which you want to configure the new hire tasks.

Add the "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" Step:

Drag and drop the "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" step into the desired location within the process flow. This step can be placed at any point in the onboarding process, depending on when you want these tasks to be presented to the new hire.

Configure Primary and Secondary Tasks:

To add standard tasks to the Primary New Hire Tasks step:

  1. Go to Admin Center>Manage Onboarding and Offboarding Tasks.
  2. Select the  Edit icon next to the Onboarding program. The Manage Onboarding Program page displays.
  3. Select + Add Task or + Add a Custom Task and choose a task from the dropdown menu.
  4. For the Required task field, select Yes or No.
  5. For the Assigned to field, select a name from the dropdown menu.
  6. Select Done to save. The task is added to the Primary New Hire Task section.

To add a task to the Secondary New Hire Tasks section, select the task and drag it to the Secondary New Hire Tasks section. The order of the tasks in this section is the order in which they appear in the Onboarding program. You can select a task and drag it to the desired order within the program.

You can also attach a New Hire Tasks Document Flow step to either the Primary New Hire Tasks step or the Secondary New Hire Tasks step to collect signatures for any custom task. Custom tasks requiring a signature must be added to the new hire task step (either primary or secondary) and attached to the Document Flow for New Hire Tasks. The Document Flow for the New Hire Tasks step is triggered upon completion of the custom tasks assigned in this step.

If you attached a Document Flow for the New Hire Tasks step to either the Primary New Hire Tasks step or the Secondary New Hire Tasks step, you need to adjust the Assign Forms (OnboardingAssignFormRule) rule to ensure the forms are assigned based on the step with the attached Document Flow for New Hire Tasks.

Save and Deploy the Process Variant:

Once the configuration is complete, save and deploy the process variant. The new hire tasks will now be presented according to the defined steps and categories during the onboarding process.

Conclusion

The "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" step in SuccessFactors Onboarding represents a significant enhancement in how organizations can manage and personalize the onboarding process. By categorizing tasks into Primary and Secondary, HR teams can ensure that new hires receive the right information and complete essential activities promptly. This not only improves the onboarding experience for new employees but also helps organizations maintain compliance and drive engagement from day one.

Note: The Secondary New Hire Tasks steps can’t be added before the Personal Data Collection step, after the Final Review Step, or after the Primary New Hire Tasks step.

entry.title: Re: Block for accounting of additions of an AF with zero...

You may find further information in Logical Field Group Assignment in Validation and Substitution Rules

Alternatively, an ABAP report shall be created by the local SAP Consultant.

entry.title: Re: Load Balancing
entry.content:
Firstly, the WEBDISPATCHER is only used for the HTTP/HTTP connections. For RFC or SAP GUI ones, the Message Server natively handles them (if the LOGON SERVERS groups are configured correctly, TCODE SMLG).

By default, SAP WD uses a Weighted Round Robin strategy by default. But if you see that's not working, you can change it to Load Balancing Using Server Response Times (namely if the servers are very different in performance). Please check this link: 

https://help.sap.com/doc/saphelp_nw74/7.4.16/en-us/48/8fe37933114e6fe10000000a421937/frameset.htm

entry.title: Where can start learning how to use the performance tools in SAP Analytics Cloud?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Managing Performance in SAP Analytics Cloud learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will provide you with the knowledge and skills required to be able to successfully manage performance in SAP Analytics Cloud. You will gain insight into SAP Analytics Cloud performance tools and troubleshooting performance issues in SAP Analytics Cloud stories.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will be able to describe the different elements of SAP Analytics Cloud architecture and how they can impact on performance, and the tools used to analyze performance issues. You will be able to create stories using performance best practice, use the performance tools to analyze performance, as well as troubleshoot common performance issues related to SAP Analytics Cloud story design.

Prerequisites

  • Acquiring Basic Scripting Skills to Extend Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Picking of partial HUs from Bulk - rounding
entry.content:
Hi SAP collegues,

I have a following business requirement.

Business stores semifinish materials in WHS mainly in full pallets, mainly on bulk ST (sometimes open ST). Partial pallets are allowed in bulk 1 per bin due to ends of production or returns of not consumed stock from PSA. For business it is impossible to separate partial HUs from full HUs.

Scenario:

Mat 123 stored in bulk ST on a full pallets 21ST/PAL + one partial pallet with 17ST/PAL. Stock in same bin and in one quant.

Alt UOM for full pallets is PAL, alt UOM for partial HU is ST.

Request of that product to be delivered to PSA (request for 45 ST).

My expectation is that I have 1 WT to pick complete partial HU with 17 ST on it, then remaining qty will be rounded up to full pallets and in this case 2 full pallets will be proposed.

Material has active packspec 21st/PAL and in determination I do not have OWHT CCtC.

 

We tried so far:

rounding on WPT 4100 - 4; rounding on ST; different entries in scwm/psastage; removal rules based on QUAN asc + desc; ST determination based on quantity classification (we set quantity classification for ST = 1 and PAL = 2); multiple sort rules, filter and limits on a WOCR; tried to work with preffered UOM as PAL set on mat1.

No success so far. May I ask for any advice please as we run out of ideas. Thanks!
EWM - Master Data , EWM - Basic Functions , EWM - Goods Movement , SAP Extended Warehouse Management, EWM - Warehouse Order Processing

entry.title: Re: Error in creation material master MM01
entry.content:
Hello @Saurabh_2804 

The express message "Express document update was cancelled received from the author XXXXX" means that the update task failed. You can get the details of the failed update task in SM13. Typically update tasks fail with short dumps that can be analyzed in ST22. 

You need to verify the details in SM13 and ST22 to see what the root cause is. Please share those details so I can help more.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: 🟢 SAP Forms by Adobe
entry.content:
Hello Peter, Hello Sandra,

Does the use of Cloud ADS under RISE with SAP not pose a Data Compliance issue for clients in sensitive industries (Finances, Defense, Security, ...)?

By default, a RISE with SAP contract comes with a preconfigured ADS tenant that does NOT belong to the client's BTP Global Account. Thus, all forms need to be sent out of the Corporate Network of the client to receive a PDF as a response. That means, that highly sensitive information that resides within the S/4HANA Private Cloud instance, like a Sales Order, are sent outside the Corporate Network just for printing.

Could you please elaborate if my understanding is wrong?

Many thanks and kind regards,

Christian Sosa

entry.title: Re: Can payment be stopped after actual payment run and b...
entry.content:
Hello,

Here's the answer that the experts have provided:

"Depending on the scenario, the following happens after the payment run has occurred:

  • There is no Bank Communication Management (BCM) active (no scope item J78 Advanced Cash Payment): The payment medium is directly generated, and the payment can only be stopped by the Multi-Bank Connectivity (MBC) tenant.
  • BCM is active and
    • There is auto-approve: The payment medium is directly generated, and the payment can only be stopped by MBC.
    • There is an approval process in place: Only the very first approver can reject the whole payment batch or particular payment Items; those payments that were rejected will not be included in the payment medium.
    • The second to fourth approver can only return the whole payment batch to the first approver to change the decision and to reject the whole payment batch and/or payment Items.
  • When there is MBC, it should always be possible to stop the payment before going to bank.
  • When there is no MBC, the payment medium stays in the Manage Payment Medium app and should be processed manually.
  • When there is no MBC, but 2YM process is active, it is up to the customer to stop the payment in CPI / BTP."

I hope this helps.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: SAP BTP, Kyma runtime: APIRule migration - noAuth and jwt handlers
entry.content:
Hello everyone! In my previous blogpost, I discussed the upcoming architectural changes to the API Gateway module. For some time now, you've been able to experiment with an early implementation of the Istio-based JWT handler. Today, I am happy to say it's time to try the finished version!

Overview

The API Gateway module in version 2.4.0 includes APIRule v2alpha1 with noAuth and jwt handlers, marking a significant milestone toward achieving functional parity with the previous version. APIRule v2alpha1 is not intended for use in a productive environment but only for testing the API and migration process before the introduction of the stable APIRule in version v2. I highly recommend reading the APIRule v2alpha1 CustomResourceDefinition (CRD) documentation, subscribing to the Kyma API Gateway module What's New notifications, and watching the APIRule v2beta1 epic to track incoming changes!

For now, the zero downtown migration from APIRule v1beta1 to APIRule v2alpha1 is supported only for the following scenarios:

  • From noop, allow, or no_auth handler in version v1beta1 to the noAuth handler in version v2alpha1
  • From the jwt handler in version v1beta1 to the new configuration of the jwt handler in version v2alpha1

Any other changes may affect the availability of the exposed workload and should be done during a maintenance window. Be aware that migration from APIRule v2alpha1 to v1beta1 is not supported and might result in unwanted effects, such as misconfiguration or downtime.

In this blog post, I describe the first scenario using the noop handler as an example. If you already use the no_auth handler in APIRule v1beta1, then migration should be a piece of cake! Next, I guide you through migrating from the Oathkeeper-based jwt handler.

This tutorial assumes you have Istio and API Gateway modules added. To focus on migration, it uses an HTTPBin service, which exposes the headers' endpoint. The HTTPBin service is deployed in its own namespace. Istio is enabled in this namespace, so the workload is part of the Istio service mesh.

Migrate APIRule v1beta1 of type noop, allow, or no_auth to APIRule v2alpha1 of type noAuth

APIRules of type noop, allow,  and no_auth  look very similar, so the migration procedure for them is the same. To demonstrate the migration procedure, this paragraph uses the following APIRule of type noop:

 

apiVersion: gateway.kyma-project.io/v1beta1
kind: APIRule
metadata:
  name: httpbin
  namespace: test
spec:
  gateway: test/httpbin-gateway
  host: httpbin.example.com
  rules:
  - accessStrategies:
    - handler: noop
    methods:
    - GET
    path: /headers
  service:
    name: httpbin
    namespace: test
    port: 8000

 

The above configuration uses the noop handler to expose the HTTPBin headers' endpoint under the domain httpbin.example.com. Calling the headers' endpoint of this URL results in the following response:

 

{
  "headers": {
    "Accept": "*/*",
    "Accept-Encoding": "gzip",
    "Host": "httpbin.test.svc.cluster.local:8000",
    "Test": "true",
    "User-Agent": "curl/8.7.1"
    ...
  }
}

 

In APIRule v2alpha1, the noAuth handler replaces the noop, allow, and no_auth handlers used in APIRule v1beta1. This means that you must modify your existing APIRule so that it is a valid APIRule of type noAuth in the newer version. Let's apply the modified APIRule:

 

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: gateway.kyma-project.io/v2alpha1
kind: APIRule
metadata:
  name: httpbin
  namespace: test
spec:
  hosts:
    - httpbin.example.com
  service:
    name: httpbin
    namespace: test
    port: 8000
  gateway: test/httpbin-gateway
  rules:
    - path: /headers
      methods: ["GET"]
      noAuth: true
EOF

 

Under the hood, API Gateway Controller recognizes this change as migration to the newer version and acts accordingly. It creates resources for the new version of APIRule and waits for them to become active before deleting the corresponding resources for the old APIRule. This way, the workload is accessible all the time, and users do not experience any downtime. Notice that getting APIRule by running the command kubectl get apirule httpbin -n test -o yaml returns APIRule in version v1beta1:

 

apiVersion: gateway.kyma-project.io/v1beta1
kind: APIRule
metadata:
  name: httpbin
  namespace: test
spec:
  gateway: test/httpbin-gateway
  host: httpbin.example.com
  rules:
  - accessStrategies:
    - handler: no_auth
    methods:
    - GET
    path: /headers
  service:
    name: httpbin
    namespace: test
    port: 8000
...

 

APIRule v1beta1 is the default version in a cluster. The APIRule v2alpha1 must be requested explicitly: kubectl get apirules.v2alpha1.gateway.kyma-project.io httpbin -n test -o yaml. Once you run this command, you get APIRule in the newer version:

 

apiVersion: gateway.kyma-project.io/v2alpha1
kind: APIRule
metadata:
  name: httpbin
  namespace: test
spec:
  gateway: test/httpbin-gateway
  hosts:
  - httpbin.example.com
  rules:
  - methods:
    - GET
    noAuth: true
    path: /headers
  service:
    name: httpbin
    namespace: test
    port: 8000
...

 

Migrate APIRule v1beta1 of type jwt to APIRule v2alpha1 of type noAuth

See the following example of APIRule that includes the jwt handler :

 

apiVersion: gateway.kyma-project.io/v1beta1
kind: APIRule
metadata:
  name: httpbin
  namespace: test
spec:
  gateway: test/httpbin-gateway
  host: httpbin.example.com
  rules:
    - path: /headers
      methods:
      - GET
      accessStrategies:
      - handler: jwt
        config:
          jwks_urls:
          -  https://{IAS_TENANT}.accounts.ondemand.com/oauth2/certs
  service:
    name: httpbin
    namespace: test
    port: 8000

 

You can access the HTTPBin service using a JSON Web Token issued by your tenant of Cloud Identity Services. There's no need to issue new tokens unless they're expired. To migrate to the newer version, apply the following APIRule:

 

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: gateway.kyma-project.io/v2alpha1
kind: APIRule
metadata:
  name: httpbin
  namespace: test
spec:
  hosts:
    - httpbin.example.com
  service:
    name: httpbin
    port: 8000
  gateway: test/httpbin-gateway
  rules:
    - jwt:
        authentications:
          -  issuer: https://{YOUR_TENANT}.accounts.ondemand.com
             jwksUri: https://{YOUR_TENANT}.accounts.ondemand.com/oauth2/certs
      methods:
        - GET
      path: /headers
EOF

 

There is an additional mandatory field in the new configuration of the jwt handler called issuer. Under the hood, APIRule Controller applies Istio resources, including RequestAuthentication, which requires the issuer to be specified. You can find the issuer URL in the OIDC well-known configuration of your Cloud Identity Service tenant at https://{YOUR_TENANT}.accounts.ondemand.com/.well-known/openid-configuration.

As in the previous case, the exposed workload is available during migration.

Next steps

API Gateway 2.4.0 marked a significant milestone in the development of the stable APIRule v2, introducing the noAuth and jwt handlers. API Gateway 2.5.0 introduced request modifiers, which replace the mutators known from APIRule v1beta1, and the release 2.6.0 added support for extAuth as a migration path for remaining Oathkeeper-based handlers. The upcoming final release, API Gateway 2.7.0, will include a short host feature, completing the introduction of APIRule v2alpha1.

After the release of API Gateway 2.7.0 you will be required to start testing migration to APIRule v2alpha1. This version of APIRule is expected to be promoted to stable version v2 without significant changes. The following timeline and all dates mentioned are specific to the regular channel.

The release of API Gateway 2.7.0, scheduled for October 28, 2024, will open a testing window. During this time, you'll have the opportunity to test the functionalities of APIRule v2alpha1 and the migration procedure. Note that APIRule v2alpha1 is not intended for use in a productive environment but only for testing the API and migration process. The testing window will last around five months, from October 28, 2024, to March 31, 2025. Once the testing window is closed, the stable APIRule v2 will be introduced. The migration to version v2 will start on March 31, 2025, and end on May 12, 2025. During the migration window, you must migrate to the stable APIRule v2. The migration period will end on June 16, 2025, when APIRule v1beta1 will be deleted. After this date, only one version of APIRule will be supported.

Summary

In this blogpost, I explained the migration process to the new APIRule v2alpha1 with noAuth and jwt handlers. My team and I made sure that this process is as least disturbing as possible by introducing only necessary changes and ensuring that your applications are always available. In the next blogpost, I plan to guide you through the migration to extAuth handler. I highly recommend learning more about the introduced changes, reading the documentation, and subscribing to release notifications. In case you have any concerns or feedback, leave us a question.

entry.title: How do you deal with User Custom Fields in Story Reports?
entry.content:
Dear All,

we are intensively working on story reports to replace the old canvas reports in the report center.

To our great disappointment we realized that user custom fields of SF LRN  can only be used in story reports, if for each value in the custom field all available locales are filled. 

We have the users' cost centers as well as the department information as custom fields. And we see no way in filling the value locales automatically, neither via sf user connector, nor via data import or any other connector file. SAP recommends to add the locales manually - a great suggestion looking at thousands of cost centers and thousands of departments with a continuous change.

Do you experience the same challenge? How are you dealing with this?

Thanks, Uwe

entry.title: Asset Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
This blog post provides an overview of innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition release 2408 for maintenance management and resource scheduling capabilities as part of line of business Asset Management.

Table of Contents

Maintenance Management 

  • Mass Change Custom Fields of Maintenance Plans and Maintenance Items (‌‌demo video)
  • Perform Maintenance Jobs app - “As-found” Condition recording + Service details (demo video)
  • My Maintenance Requests app – Filter for Maintenance Requests (demo video)
  • Support Equipment PRT in UI, OData API and Data Migration Cockpit (‌‌demo video)
  • Harmonized Document Management (HDM) Attachment UI Adoption in Change Maintenance Notification app (demo video)

Resource Scheduling 

  • Work Center Utilization Calculations shown in User Time Zone (demo video)demo video)

SAP Maintenance Assistant 

  • Install and Dismantle Equipment (demo video)

SAP Asset Performance Management 

More Information / Additional links 

 

For a focussed start, check out my video highlighting the following innovations:

  • Perform Maintenance Jobs app - “As-found” Condition recording
  • Mass Change Custom Fields of Maintenance Plans and Maintenance Items
  • My Maintenance Requests app – Filter for Maintenance Requests

Video 1: Highlights for Asset Management in the 2408 release

 

Maintenance Management 

Mass Change Custom Fields of Maintenance Plans and Maintenance Items

With the Manage Maintenance Plans (F5325) app and the Manage Maintenance Items (F5356) app, you can now change custom fields of multiple maintenance plans and maintenance items respectively by choosing the Mass Change button. Previously, you were only able to perform mass changes on predefined fields. But now, you can also perform mass changes on custom fields that you have created. This flexibility allows you to tailor the system to your specific needs and streamline your maintenance processes.
In the video below I show you the mass change of custom fields in the Manage Maintenance Plans app:

Video 2: Mass Change Custom Fields of Maintenance Plans

In addition, you can do the same using the Maintenance Plan and Maintenance Item OData API.

(🔙Table of Contents)

 

Perform Maintenance Jobs app - Enhancements

Recording of the “As-found” Condition

As a Maintenance Technician, the Perform Maintenance Jobs app allows you to review, execute, and report on dispatched jobs. Until now, Malfunction Data was only available if the order had an associated notification. However, we've now added the functionality to record the as-found condition of the maintained asset in a proactive maintenance job, even when the maintenance order has no assigned maintenance notification, such as when the order is generated directly from a maintenance plan.
In addition, this enhancement enables you also in reactive maintenance to record failure data against a dedicated notification in case the technical object on the order operation differs from the technical object on the order header.

Service details enablement

Furthermore, the Perform Maintenance Jobs app now displays lean service details and its status for an operation. This enhancement not only improves user experience but also boosts the recording of asset reliability data.

Watch the video below in which I show you the enhancements of the Perform Maintenance Jobs app. 

Video 3: 'Perform Maintenance Jobs' app - “As-found” Condition recording and Services details

(🔙Table of Contents)

My Maintenance Requests app – Filter for Maintenance Requests

With the My Maintenance Requests app, you can now filter, group, or sort maintenance requests by various parameters such as planning plant, planner group, main work center, maintenance plant, technical object, location, and more. For this purpose we added the filter bar to the app. This powerful filtering capability enables you to quickly find the information you need, improving your efficiency and productivity.
In the next video I'm stepping into the shoes of a maintenance planner who is searching for maintenance requests with notification processing context 'Emergency Work'.

Video 4: 'My Maintenance Requests' app – Filter for Maintenance Requests

(🔙Table of Contents)

Support Equipment PRT in UI, OData API and Data Migration Cockpit

You can now do the following if the category of the equipment is P (Production resources/tools):

  • Create, view, and update production resources/tools (PRT) using the Web Dynpro apps Create Technical Object and Change Technical Object.
  • View production resources/tools data for equipment using the Fiori app Find Technical Object and the Web Dynpro app Display Technical Object.
  • The service API_EQUIPMENT enables you to create, read and update PRT data for a Equipment.
  • Equipment Data Migration Cockpit (DMC) template is enhanced to support PRT data.

In the next video I show you how the SAP Fiori app Find Technical Object supports now Equipment PRT:

Video 5: 'Find Technical Object' app supports Equipment PRT

(🔙Table of Contents)

Harmonized Document Management (HDM) Attachment UI Adoption in Change Maintenance Notification app

Now you can use reusable Harmonized Document Management (HDM) attachment UI adoption in the Change Maintenance Notification web dynpro application. If you choose in the menu the Manage Attachments option as shown in the next video, a new window will open with the SAP Fiori HDM attachment UI. Here you can upload or assign documents or add links. If you upload for instance a picture, a new Document Info Record (DIR) is created and the uploaded picture is uploaded to the Maintenance Notification.

Video 6: 'Change Maintenance Notification' app - Harmonized Document Management (HDM) Attachment

(🔙Table of Contents)

Resource Scheduling 

Work Center Utilization Calculations shown in User Time Zone

Previously work center utilization was calculated based on the system time zone (UTC), which did not allow
users working in other time zones to see the data in a meaningful way, especially with maintenance operations that are planned in night shifts. The data is now displayed according to the user’s specific time zone. This applies to the following apps: 

  • Resource Scheduling for Maintenance Planners
  • Manage Work Center Utilization
  • Maintenance Scheduling Board

Watch the next video that shows how the Operation Planned Start and Operation Planned End date and time are displayed according to the user’s specific time zone.

Video 7: Work Center Utilization Calculations shown in User Time Zone

(🔙Table of Contents)

SAP Maintenance Assistant 

Install and Dismantle Equipment

SAP Maintenance Assistant is the mobile maintenance execution solution for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Watch the next video to learn the improvements to user experience in SAP Maintenance Assistant app and the new capability to install and dismantle equipment.

Video 8: SAP Maintenance Assistant - Install and Dismantle Equipment

This demo was presented by Zack Frazier during the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series. Check out also Zack's brand new blog post SAP Maintenance Assistant 2408 Now Available – What’s New?.

(🔙Table of Contents)

 

SAP Asset Performance Management 

SAP Asset Performance Management empowers asset owners, plant managers, and reliability engineers to optimize maintenance strategies, measure and improve asset performance, and follow through on your sustainability objectives. Kate Dowle shares the latest news around SAP Asset Performance Management in her blog post series. Check out her brand new blog post What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2408 that provides videos on our latest updates, including Joule's AI-powered integration with SAP Help Portal (part of the Early Adopter Access initiative) and enhanced features like global operating contexts in strategy assessments for classes, and time-saving enhancements to FMEA assessments.

(🔙Table of Contents)

 

More Information / Additional links 

You will find a collection of SAP S/4HANA Cloud product centric blog posts from Markus Oertelt and me here:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Asset Management – The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Manufacturing & Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • Discrete Industries in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - The Link Collection

 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X. 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series! 

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a xx-minute-long recording and a presentation for LoB/Industry/Topic as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. entry.title: Quedada SAP - post SAP TechEd y Devtoberfest 2024 en Honest Greens Castellana
entry.content:
¡Hola miembros de la comunidad SAP en Madrid!

¿Que les parece si quedamos nuevamente? Pero esta vez para hablar un poco de lo que fue TechEd 2024 y Devtoberfest 2024.

La Quedada SAP en Madrid se llevará a cabo el 24 de octubre de 2024 a las 18:30 hora local en el Honest Greens de Castellana, que esta muy cerca de la nueva oficina de SAP.

La idea es juntarnos y compartir un poco sobre todo lo que es el mundo SAP. En esta ocasion compartir experiencia de dos eventos importantes que acaban de concluir en el mundo SAP.... como lo son SAP TechEd y Devtoberfest. Aparte de esto, tambien coincide que estas fechas esta @MustafaBensan (SAP Champion) por Madrid y se nos unira. Por si no saben quien es Mustafa pueden ver este blog post - https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-business-technology-platform-blog-posts/meet-and-greet-interview-with-mustafa-bensan/ba-p/128549.

Por favor, haznos saber si participarás en la quedada haciendo clic en la seccion de RSVP. No tenemos una reserva como tal en el espacio pero es un lugar bastante amplio y podriamos juntar alli un par de mesas. Nota: Cada uno es responsable de comprar su propia comida o bebida si lo desea.

¡Nos vemos pronto!

-----

¡Hola SAP Community members in Madrid!

How about we meet again? But this time to chat about SAP TechEd and Devtoberfest 2024.

The SAP Meetup in Madrid will take place on October 24, 2024, at 18:30 local time at Honest Greens in Castellana, which is very close to the new SAP office.

The idea is to get together and share a bit about everything in the SAP world. This time around, share our experiences about two recent events in the SAP world - SAP TechEd and Devtoberfest 2024. Also, SAP Champion @MustafaBensan is in town and he will be joining us :-). In case you don't know who Mustafa is... check out this blog post - https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-business-technology-platform-blog-posts/meet-and-greet-interview-with-mustafa-bensan/ba-p/128549.

Please let us know if you will be participating in the meetup by clicking on the RSVP section. We do not have a reservation in the space, but it is fairly large, and we could put a couple of tables together there. Note: Everyone is responsible for purchasing their own food or drinks if they wish to.

See you soon!

Antonio Maradiaga
Developer Advocate @ SAP

No ”Au contraire” there, your link proves my point.  The only place your link uses the incorrect ”on-premise“ is in the line that you quoted.  Elsewhere in the link the correct “on-premises” is written 44 times and the often written (incorrectly) as “on-premise” is nowhere else except your once quoted example.

https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/On-premises_software
'(abbreviated to on-prem, and often written as "on-premise")'

Remember, there is no such thing as "on-premise" in real English.
"Premise" is concept.
"Premises" is a location.
"https://collectivecontent.agency/2018/04/19/should-i-say-on-premise-or-on-premises-it/"

entry.title: Update User With Sap Bo Rest Api
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to convert below java code to c# code using sap bo rest apis

Java code;

  
  public void updateUser(IUser boUser, BOUser repoUser, boolean isDisabled) throws Exception {
        if (repoUser != null) {
      boUser.setFullName(repoUser.getFullName());
      boUser.setEmailAddress(repoUser.getStrEmail());
    } 
    
    if (this.sourceProp != null)
      boUser.properties().setProperty("SI_DATA", this.sourceProp.getValue()); 
	  
    boUser.setPreferredViewingLocale(Locale.ENGLISH);
    boUser.setLocale(Locale.ENGLISH);
    boUser.setConnection(repoUser.getBoUserType());
    boUser.setPasswordExpiryAllowed(false);
    IUserAliases aliases = boUser.getAliases();
    IUserAlias alias = (IUserAlias)aliases.toArray()[0];
    alias.setDisabled(isDisabled);    
  }

 I researched and found SAP BO USER UPDATE adress. However, in the java code above, while updating the user, operations such as setPreferredViewingLocale, setConnection, setPasswordExpiryAllowed and alias.setDisabled are performed, but these fields are not available in the rest api and update address I sent. Are the rest API equivalents of these update fields different? How can I do the same operations as in Java code with rest api in c#?

entry.title: Re: How to properly mock transition messages using "ui5-m...
entry.content:
Hi Dominik,
may I ask you if you are referring to SAP Fiori elements for OData v2 or v4? As you are talking about function imports I guess you are using a SAP Fiori elements for OData v2 application, right? (SAP Fiori elements for OData v4 does not support function imports).

Indeed there's also a slightly difference between v2 and v4 in regards of the message handling. While v4 only shows unbound messages in the dialog and bound messages in the message popover (and doesn't care if it's a transition message or not), v2 shows only the transition messages in the dialog. Thus, without having the transition flag set, the message would not be shown in the dialog (only in the message popover of the object page once you are in the edit mode).

This sounds like an issue with our mockserver, or at least with our documentation. Therefore, can I ask you to report an issue here:
https://github.com/SAP/open-ux-odata/issues

Thanks and best regards,
Marcel

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud Manage Outbound Deliveries – VL0...

Hello,

thank you for your question. Have you also tried to use the Manage Outbound Deliveries App (F0867A)? This app allows you to add "Freight Order" as filter criteria to search for deliveries. In addition the "Freight Order" is available as parameter in the Schedule Goods Issue for Deliveries app, in case you would like to automate the Goods Issue Posting. Looking forward to your feedback. Thank you!

Best regards,

Darius Nowak

Cloud ERP Product Management - Logistics

entry.title: Power Up Your Networking and Knowledge in the Utilities and Energy Industry with SAP Community
entry.content:
Dear Utilities and Oil, Gas, and Energy Enthusiasts and Professionals. 

Are you looking to stay ahead of the latest trends and developments in the utilities and energy industry? Are you keen on networking with other professionals, gaining insights from industry experts, and expanding your knowledge base? If your answer is yes, then belonging to the SAP for Utilities and Oil, Gas, and Energy Community is a MUST for you!

There are several ways to engage with us:

The SAP Community is a thriving platform for professionals in the utilities and energy sector to come together, share ideas, ask questions, and stay updated on the latest innovations and best practices. Being active in the SAP Community will provide you access to a wealth of resources and opportunities to connect with like-minded individuals.

Join the SAP Utilities Group 

 

 

Join the SAP Oil, Gas, and Energy Group

 

 

By signing up for the newsletter, you will have access to valuable resources, including articles, blogs, webinars, whitepapers that cover a wide range of topics relevant to the utilities and energy industry. Whether you're interested in learning about the latest technologies, industry trends, or best practices, you'll find a wealth of information to fuel your professional growth.

Sign up for the Utilities Newsletter

 

 

Sign up for the Oil, Gas, and Energy Newsletter

 

 

Attending our events, particularly in-person, will greatly enhance your networking opportunities. In-person events offer a fantastic platform to connect with industry peers, thought leaders, and experts. By actively engaging in the community, you'll have the opportunity to participate in meaningful discussions, share ideas, contribute to the knowledge base, and establish valuable connections that can advance your career and business. 

Save the date for the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference in Rotterdam from April 8-10, 2025 

Stay updated: In a fast-paced industry like utilities and energy, staying abreast of the latest developments is crucial. The SAP for Energy and Utilities Community channels help you stay updated on the latest news, product updates, and industry trends, ensuring that you're always ahead of the curve.

So, what are you waiting for? Join the SAP Community, sign up for the newsletter, and start enjoying the benefits of being an active participant in this vibrant community. Whether you're a customer, partner, or just interested in the utilities and energy sector for any reason, there's something for everyone in the SAP Community.

Watch the video 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

We can't wait to connect with you!

entry.title: Re: Business Process Model Connector (BPMC) Sync Solman 7.2 >>Signavio
entry.content: The Solman Connector (Business Process Model Connector) enables the import of initial folder structures, processes, and process step references/originals to Signavio. Attributes for processes and process step references/originals can also be mapped (see SAP help: https://help.sap.com/docs/signavio-process-transformation-suite/business-process-model-connector/mapping-attributes?locale=en-US). However, there are limitations regarding the types of fields supported for mapping. An upcoming update, expected in its initial version by the end of 2024 (see external Roadmap: https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=F71E8E8587BC1EEE9FB5474ED9D6152D&q=business%2520process%2520model%2520connector&range=CURRENT-LAST#;INNO=45AB3E795FB61EEF91BB8F400491B94F), will allow synchronization of executables, with further enhancements anticipated in 2025 (not yet visible on public roadmap). As of now, this feature is not available. All other attachments, such as documents, test steps, etc., remain exclusively managed in SolMan and are not transferred to Signavio.
